description_long: This object consists of three distinct traffic signs mounted on individual metal posts. The leftmost sign is a yellow diamond with a black pictogram of a person slipping and the words 'ARE NO MORE' in red graffiti. The center sign is an octagonal red stop sign with the word 'STOP' and additional graffiti, including 'NO' and 'SEE'. The rightmost sign is rectangular, white with black and red text, reading 'KEEP LEFT' and 'ONE WAY', also heavily marked with graffiti. All signs show signs of weathering, vandalism, and wear, with visible rust and faded paint. The posts are dark, likely metal, and appear sturdy, supporting the signs at a height suitable for visibility to pedestrians and drivers. The overall appearance suggests these signs have been in place for some time in an urban environment prone to vandalism.
description: A group of three weathered, graffiti-covered traffic signs on metal posts, including a stop sign, a warning sign, and a directional sign. The signs show significant wear and vandalism, with faded paint and rusted posts.
description_view_0: Front view showing all three signs clearly: a yellow warning sign, a red stop sign, and a white directional sign, each with graffiti and weathering.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the signs and posts, with minimal detail visible except the depth of the sign panels.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the signs, all in a dark, unpainted finish, with the mounting hardware and posts visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness of the signs and posts, with some red graffiti visible on the edge of the rightmost sign.
attribute_sign_text:
ARE NO MORE
NO STOP SEE
KEEP LEFT ONE WAY
attribute_symbol_or_pictogram: Slipping person pictogram on the leftmost sign; octagonal stop sign shape in the center.
